title: Fix missing /symptom-checker route warning — remove standalone route, keep Symptom Checker in Health tab

requirements.summary: Remove any standalone /symptom-checker route entry from the root router configuration to eliminate the missing-route console warning
acceptanceCriteria: No console warning about a missing /symptom-checker route when the app starts.
App.tsx (or equivalent router file) contains no <Route>, stack screen, or tab entry pointing to /symptom-checker.
No unused imports of a standalone SymptomChecker route component remain in the router file.

file_operations.description: Remove any standalone route definition for /symptom-checker from the router configuration. Check all route trees (public, admin, doctor, phlebotomist) and remove any Route component with path='/symptom-checker' or similar. Remove any associated imports of a SymptomChecker page component if present.

requirements.summary: Ensure the Symptom Checker component continues to function correctly as an embedded component inside the Health tab's Digital Health section
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to the Health tab displays the Digital Health section with the Symptom Checker embedded and fully functional.
No broken component rendering or null-reference errors related to the Symptom Checker after route removal.
The bottom tab navigator contains exactly: Home, Bookings, Health, Subscriptions, Profile — with no extra tab for Symptom Checker.

file_operations.description: Verify that the Symptom Checker component is properly embedded within the Health tab's Digital Health section as an internal component (not a routed page). Ensure it renders correctly without requiring navigation to a separate route. If the component relies on route parameters or navigation state, refactor it to work as an embedded component within the page structure.

requirements.summary: Remove all orphaned imports, navigation links, and redirect logic that reference the removed /symptom-checker route across the entire frontend codebase
acceptanceCriteria: A project-wide search for the string 'symptom-checker' (as a route path) returns no results in navigation or routing files.
No TypeScript/JavaScript import errors related to a removed SymptomChecker route module.
App compiles and runs with a clean console — no warnings or errors related to the symptom-checker route.

file_operations.description: Remove any navigation links, useNavigate('/symptom-checker') calls, or menu items that reference the /symptom-checker route. Ensure navigation logic does not attempt to route to this path.
Remove any redirect logic, route guards, or navigation references to /symptom-checker. Check for any conditional navigation or link components that point to this route.
Remove any footer navigation links to /symptom-checker. Verify no Link or anchor tags reference this route path.
